questions.q: Which family of instruments produces sound by vibrating strings?
questions.explanation: String instruments (violin, cello, guitar, harp) produce sound through vibrating strings. Brass uses air columns; woodwind uses reeds or air; percussion uses striking or shaking.

questions.q: What is the lowest-pitched string instrument commonly found in orchestras?
questions.explanation: The double bass (or bass viol) is the lowest string instrument in orchestras, typically tuned E-A-D-G. The cello is higher; violin and viola are much higher.

questions.q: How many strings does a standard violin have?
questions.explanation: A standard violin has four strings tuned G-D-A-E from lowest to highest. Violas, cellos, and basses have four strings tuned differently in their respective ranges.

questions.q: Which woodwind instrument has a double reed?
questions.explanation: The oboe uses a double reed (two pieces of cane vibrating together). The clarinet and saxophone use single reeds; the flute has no reed, using air-blown tone holes.

questions.q: What is the typical range of a French horn in orchestral music?
questions.explanation: The French horn sits in a tenor-alto range, spanning roughly three octaves. Though it can extend higher or lower, its primary orchestral role is in the middle-to-upper-middle range.

questions.q: Which percussion instrument is struck with mallets and has tuned metal bars?
questions.explanation: The vibraphone (or vibes) has tuned metal bars that resonate sympathetically, with a motor-driven vibrato system. The xylophone has wooden bars; timpani are kettle drums; triangle is a single bar.

questions.q: The trombone produces different pitches primarily by using which mechanism?
questions.explanation: The trombone uses a slide to lengthen or shorten the air column, producing different pitches. Trumpets and French horns use valves; woodwinds use keys or finger holes.

questions.q: What is the standard tuning of a four-string bass guitar?
questions.explanation: A four-string bass is tuned E-A-D-G, the same note names as a guitar's lowest four strings but one octave lower. Five and six-string basses add lower notes (B or F♯).

questions.q: Which brass instrument is the highest-pitched in standard orchestral use?
questions.explanation: The trumpet produces the highest pitches among standard brass instruments. The French horn is lower; trombones and tubas are progressively lower in range.

questions.q: The pipe organ is controlled by which mechanism to change pitches and tone colors?
questions.explanation: Pipe organs use a keyboard (keys) and stops (handles controlling which pipes sound), allowing the player to select pitches and timbal variety. No other woodwind, brass, or string instrument works this way.
